project_requirements: When battery temperature rises above threshold, charging current must derate safely.
The vehicle must publish a diagnostic trouble code when thermal sensor data is invalid.
Driver-visible charging status must remain accurate during derating and recovery.
safety_notes: Charging derating must enter fail-safe mode if temperature sampling is unavailable.
DTC reporting and recovery state transitions require validation.
mr_title: Fix charging derate regression for high battery temperature
mr_description: Updates battery thermal logic, charging control state machine, and cluster charging status mapping after a field regression.
acceptance_criteria: Charging current derates correctly when battery temperature exceeds threshold.
Invalid thermal sensor values set a diagnostic trouble code.
Cluster charging status reflects derated and recovered states correctly.
diff_summary: Changed battery thermal evaluation, charging state transitions, diagnostic reporting, and cluster signal mapping.

bug_history.summary: Field issue: charging current did not derate when thermal sensor latency increased.
Battery ECU recovered too early after over-temperature and caused charge oscillation.

test_coverage_notes: HIL coverage exists for nominal charging but thermal sensor fault coverage is weak.
Diagnostics regression pack does not cover all recovery transitions.
